incident.name: Garden Grove MMA Tank Leak
incident.status_headline: OCFA confirmed Monday morning May 25 that the catastrophic BLEVE explosion threat is eliminated after tank pressure was relieved; ~50,000 residents remain under evacuation orders while a smaller explosion or leak risk persists.

evacuation.boundary_text: South to Trask Avenue, north to Ball Road, west to Valley View Street, east to Dale Street

official_statements.text: The threat of a BLEVE (Boiling Liquid Expanding Vapor Explosion) at the GKN Aerospace site is now off the table. Crews worked overnight to confirm pressure in the tank was releasing after a crack was discovered; tank temperature is currently at 93°F and continuing to decline.

Crisis is not averted. We still have potential for an explosion that is not as bad as a BLEVE, but it is still a threat. Areas outside the evacuation zone are currently considered completely safe and day-to-day activities can continue as normal.
Declared a state of emergency in Orange County and requested a Federal Emergency Declaration from President Trump. 785 state and local first responders are deployed to Orange County.
24 air quality monitors have been deployed around the evacuation zone; air quality levels remain completely normal.

schools_closed: Garden Grove Unified School District (Barker, Bryant, Carver, Enders, Garden Park, Lawrence, Wakeham, Patton elementaries; Bell and Alamitos intermediates; Pacifica and Rancho Alamitos high schools — closed through May 27)
Westminster School District (Fryberger Elementary, Sequoia Elementary — closed indefinitely)
Magnolia School District (Robert M. Pyles STEM Academy — closed indefinitely)
Savanna School District (all campuses — closed week of May 25, reopen targeted June 1)
OCDE ACCESS Program (3 impacted sites on remote instruction)
